reasoning: Short-term stock movements approximate a random walk, setting a strong base rate near 0.50. However, Gilead (GILD) has seen a sharp 15-17% run-up in the past two weeks leading into the end of January, pushing its price to roughly $141. The initial time series analysis expects some mean reversion, yielding a model-estimated probability of ~0.39-0.45 for exceeding the January 30 close price. Furthermore, Gilead reports Q4 2025 earnings on February 10. The first resolution date (February 8, effectively settling on Friday, February 6) occurs immediately before earnings, a period when investors are likely to take profits on recent gains rather than push the stock to new highs. This justifies a slight bearish lean for the first date (0.44). The second resolution date (March 3) will be shaped heavily by the results of the February 10 earnings report. Over a longer horizon, volatility cuts both ways but stock indices tend to drift upwards. Following the rule that uncertainty increases with the forecast horizon, the estimate for March 3 is pulled closer to the 0.50 coin-flip mark (0.48).
